source: Crosswalk authored by Saaksh from the MSCI ESG Ratings Key Issue Framework (10 themes / 35 Key Issues across 3 pillars) and the S&P Global Corporate Sustainability Assessment (CSA), which underpins the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices (DJSI). Mapped at the BRSR principle level — the granularity at which these ratings actually assess companies.

msci.name: MSCI ESG Ratings
msci.what: A risk-based rating (CCC to AAA) measuring a company's resilience to financially material ESG risks. Built from 35 Key Issues; 7–15 are scored per sub-industry.

djsi.name: S&P Global CSA / DJSI
djsi.what: A best-in-class score from the annual Corporate Sustainability Assessment (CSA); the top performers per industry enter the Dow Jones Sustainability Indices.

mappings.note: BRSR P1 anti-bribery, board oversight and penalty disclosures feed the Governance pillar that MSCI scores for every company.
LCA, recycled-input and EPR data support MSCI product-impact issues and the CSA Product Stewardship criterion.
BRSR P3 safety, benefits and training metrics map almost one-to-one to the CSA Human Capital criteria.
Structured stakeholder identification and materiality underpin the CSA Materiality criterion that gates much of the assessment.
BRSR P5 human-rights due diligence and supplier audits feed MSCI supply-chain issues and the CSA Human Rights criterion.
The richest overlap: BRSR P6 energy, GHG, water and waste data directly drive both MSCI's Climate/Natural-Capital themes and the CSA Environmental dimension.
BRSR P7 trade-association and advocacy disclosures map to the CSA Policy Influence criterion — increasingly scrutinised for climate-policy alignment.

msci_key_issues: Access to Finance / Health Care / Communications
mappings.note: CSR, local employment and community-impact data support MSCI social-opportunity issues and the CSA community criteria.
BRSR P9 data-privacy, product-safety and grievance disclosures feed MSCI Product-Liability issues and the CSA cyber & customer criteria.
